rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('first algorithm to combine...') and gap statement exist but are candidate targets for sharpening depending on which interpretation of 'stronger' applies; the framing of what GoalAniso unifies vs. what prior threads already touched is contestable.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact asserts benchmark superiority (41%, 28% fewer DoF) and a 'first' claim, both of which must be tightly aligned with the stated evidence; the Formaggia & Perotto (2003) description ('element-wise anisotropic DWR estimators on internal-layer benchmarks') creates a surface-level tension with the novelty claim that needs evidence-backed resolution.
rationale.R1.4: Formaggia & Perotto (2003) appears to occupy closely adjacent scientific territory—the delta between their work and GoalAniso (metric construction from dual-solution gradients vs. primal Hessian, coupling to a goal functional) is not crisply articulated; strengthening the text materially requires adjudicating this gap.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make this stronger' reliably activates prose-level improvement: sentence economy, precision of technical language, rhetorical momentum from gap statement through results.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you mean primarily improving prose quality and rhetorical force (sentence- and paragraph-level rewriting), or sharpening the scientific argument — specifically the novelty claim relative to Formaggia & Perotto (2003) and the evidence that supports it?
